(Payment cannot be accepted unless these requirements are satisfied) DoTDirect Let us come to you anytime, anywhere. Signing up to DoTDirect is now easier than ever. www.transport.wa.gov.au/dotdlrect EASY WAYS TO PAY Access our secure site www.transport.wa.gov.au and click on the DoTDirect services point to register or login. A DoTDirect online account is a free personalised account where you are able to manage all information relating to your personal vehicle licence and driver's licence, as well as pay all bills associated with these licences. You are now able to also view your boats and pay boat registration renewals through your DoTDirect online account. Telephone & Internet Banking - BPAY Contact your bank or financial institution to make this payment from your cheque, savings, debit or transaction account. For more information: www.bpay.com.au. BPAY payments must be made in FULL. Payments for invalid amounts or payments past the due by date will be rejected and returned. Access our secure transaction site www.transport.wa.gov.au/dvs and dick on the Online payments button for payment by Mastercard or VISA. (24hr service). Telephone 1300 665 322 for payment by Mastercard or VISA. (24hr service). Detach PAYMENT ADVICE and mail with cheque or money order to Department of Transport Payment Centre. GPO Box K777 PERTH WA 6B42. Cheques are accepted subjed to being met on first presentation. Make cheques or money orders payable to "Department of Transport". Payment must be made in person when a new photograph is required. You must present this account INTACT together with proof of identity. For further information and payment locations visit our website: www.transportwa.gov.au/dvs. PROOF OF IDENTITY i When renewing your driver's licence and having your photograph taken you will need to produce one form of primary and one form of secondary | identification. One of your identification documents must show your current signature. The following are acceptable forms of identification. "Acceptable forms of Primary Identification: - Australian citizenship or naturalisation document or immigration papers issued by the Department of Immigration and Border Protection. - Australian passport which has not expired for more than two years. - Consular photo identity card issued by the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade. - Current Document of Identity issued by the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade. - Current overseas passport. »Original copy of a birth certificate (not an extract). - Photographic licence issued by an Australian driver licensing authority which has not expired for more than two years. - Western Australian Photo card. - Western Australian Proof of Age card (not an 18+ card). "Acceptable forms of Secondary Identification: - Australian utilities account or notice (for example gas. water, electricity or phone bill) that is less than six months old. - Certificate or statement of accomplishment or enrolment from a recognised educational institution. - Confirmation of electoral enrolment letter Issued by the Australian Electoral Commission not more than two years old. - Current Australian EFTPOS or Credit Card. «Current photographic Australian Defence Force identification card. - Current WA Recreational Skippers Ticket. - Current WA vehicle licence. - Current WA working with Children Card. - Medicare Card. - Passbook or account statement from a financial institution not more than 12 months old. »WA photographic student identity card. 'Subject to variation. A full list of acceptable Identification documents are available online at www.transport.wa.gov.au/dvs. MANDATORY REPORTING OF MEDICAL CONDITIONS It is now mandatory for all drivers in Western Australia to notify Driver & Vehicle Services (DVS) in writing, of any permanent or long-term medical condition that is likely to impair their ability to control a motor vehicle. Further information can be located on our website www.transportwa.gov.au/dvs or by calling 1300 852 722. CHANGE OF ADDRESS If you have changed your address, you must notify the Department of Transport in writing within 21 days. Notification of change of address can be completed online at www.transport.wa.gov.au/dvs or alternatively the details may be faxed to 1300 669 995. Please provide your full name, date of birth, previous and current address, driver's licence number, and all vehicle plate numbers (including trailers, motorcycles, caravans, and boats licensed in your name). PENSIONER CONCESSION INFORMATION If you hold a Pensioner Concession Card or both a Commonwealth Seniors Health Card and WA Seniors Card, you may be eligible for a concession on your driver licence fee. For further information on concession entitlements visit www.transport.wa.gov.au/dvs or call 13 11 56. Concession entitlements must be applied for prior to the payment of the driver's licence renewal otherwise full payment is required. wwvu You can apply for a concession at all Driver and Vehicle Services (DVS) centre, regional Department of Transport (DoT) office or authorised agent. Internal use only
